subject: Physics
title: Neutron Stars: City-Sized Giants
description: A quick win: understand Neutron Stars: City-Sized Giants.

hook.text: A neutron star packs the mass of our Sun into a sphere just 20 kilometers across—denser than an atomic nucleus.

buildup.text: After a supernova, the core collapses until protons and electrons merge into neutrons. Only neutron pressure holds it up.

discovery.text: A teaspoon of neutron star material weighs about 6 billion tons—the weight of a mountain.

twist.text: Some neutron stars spin hundreds of times per second, sweeping beams of radiation like cosmic lighthouses.

climax.text: These pulsars are so regular that they were initially mistaken for alien signals—called 'LGM' for 'Little Green Men.'

punchline.text: A teaspoon weighing billions of tons. Spinning 700 times a second. Nature's extremes.

quiz.question: How dense is neutron star material?
options: A teaspoon weighs about 6 billion tons
About the same density as lead
